help.title: Help — {context}
help.home.welcome.heading: Welcome to Cadence
help.home.welcome.1: Cadence is a spaced repetition app — it helps you organise your practice sessions and track what you know.
help.home.welcome.2: Create cards for the things you want to learn, organise them into decks, and study regularly.
help.home.welcome.3: The more you practise, the better Cadence understands what you know — and surfaces what you're most likely to have forgotten.
help.home.welcome.4: Works for any learning topic. A dedicated module makes it easy to import tunes directly from TheSession.
help.home.nav.heading: Navigation
help.home.nav.1: Click a folder or deck to open it.
help.home.nav.2: Drag & drop folders and decks in the sidebar to reorganise them.
help.home.nav.3: ← → in the top bar (or Alt+← / Alt+→) to navigate history.
help.home.create.heading: Creating content
help.home.create.1: + Folder / + Deck at the bottom of the sidebar to create at the root.
help.home.create.2: Use the "+ New folder" and "+ New deck" buttons inside a folder.
help.home.create.3: Click a folder or deck name directly to rename it.
help.home.dashboard.heading: Dashboard (Home only)
help.home.dashboard.1: The dashboard shows your global study streak, sessions this week, and a card map.
help.home.dashboard.2: The card map plots each card by stability (X) and ease (Y) — colour shows availability. Filter by deck using the chips above the chart.
help.home.dashboard.3: Switch the activity chart between 7 days, 1 month, and 1 year.
help.library.browse.heading: Browsing
help.library.browse.1: Search by name or tag using the search bar.
help.library.browse.2: Click a tag pill to filter by that tag — multiple tags are combined (AND).
help.library.browse.3: Two dots per card: the first shows recall score (red < 40%, yellow < 75%, green ≥ 75%), the second shows ease.
help.library.browse.4: Hover a row to see which decks the card belongs to — click a deck chip to navigate.
help.library.selection.heading: Selection & deletion
help.library.selection.1: Tick the checkbox on a row (appears on hover) to select it.
help.library.selection.2: "Select all" selects all currently filtered cards.
help.library.selection.3: The trash button deletes all selected cards from the library and all decks.
help.library.add.heading: Adding cards
help.library.add.1: "+ New card" opens the creation modal — create a blank card or import from TheSession.
help.deck.study.heading: Studying
help.deck.study.1: "Study" opens a session with the chosen strategy.
help.deck.study.2: Random — picks any unavailable card at random.
help.deck.study.3: Optimal — picks the card with the highest urgency (importance × forgetting).
help.deck.study.4: Stochastic — weighted random, balancing urgency and variety.
help.deck.manage.heading: Managing cards
help.deck.manage.1: Drag a card row to reorder it in the deck.
help.deck.manage.2: Click the ×N importance badge to override a card's weight for this deck.
help.deck.manage.3: Click the broken-link icon to remove a card from the deck (card is not deleted).
help.deck.manage.4: Click a card name to open its detail view.
help.deck.bar.heading: Deck metrics
help.deck.bar.1: Recall (R): red < 40%, yellow < 75%, green ≥ 75%. Stability: retention window in days. Ease: how naturally the card is recalled.
help.deck.bar.2: A card is skipped during study when its recall score exceeds your recall threshold (Settings).
help.card.edit.heading: Editing
help.card.edit.1: Click the card name to rename it inline.
help.card.edit.2: Click a tag to rename it; hover a tag and click ✕ to remove it.
help.card.edit.3: Type in the "+" input to add a new tag (Enter or comma to confirm).
help.card.edit.4: Click the broken-link icon on a deck chip to remove the card from that deck.
help.card.edit.5: Click "+" next to the deck chips to add the card to another deck.
help.card.fsrs.heading: FSRS — Recall window
help.card.fsrs.1: The recall window shows how long after a review the card stays above your recall threshold.
help.card.fsrs.2: A longer recall window means the card is solidly learned.
help.card.history.heading: Review history
help.card.history.1: Click "+" in the review history to manually log a past session.
help.card.history.2: Hover an entry and click ✕ to remove it.
help.study.rating.heading: Rating a card
help.study.rating.1: ✗ Again (1) — you did not remember it at all.
help.study.rating.2: △ Hard (2) — you recalled it with difficulty.
help.study.rating.3: ○ Good (3) — correct recall with some effort.
help.study.shortcuts.heading: Keyboard shortcuts
help.study.shortcuts.2: Tab — skip without rating (disabled when only one card remains).
help.study.shortcuts.3: Esc — exit the session and return to the deck.
help.study.fsrs.heading: FSRS algorithm
help.study.fsrs.1: Each rating updates the card's Stability (S) and Difficulty (D).
help.study.fsrs.2: Higher grades increase stability — the card will be shown less often.
help.study.fsrs.3: "Failed" triggers a forgetting reset, strongly reducing stability.
help.study.fsrs.4: Cards above your recall threshold are skipped automatically.
